{{Quote|Morvis, pass the word for Chief Viddarie. See if he can find some binders for our young guest.|Admiral Saul Karath, calling for Viddarie to restrain Zayne Carrick|<ref name="kotor15" />}}
'''Viddarie''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was, by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3963 BBY]], the security [[Chief/Legends|chief]] on the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] [[Inexpugnable-class tactical command ship|battleship]] [[Courageous (Inexpugnable-class)|''Courageous'']], serving under [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]]. Viddarie was present on the ''Courageous'' when the ship traveled, with a [[Battle Group Serroco|battle group]], to protect the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Serroco]] from attack during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans. While the ''Courageous'' [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] Serroco, fugitive former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Zayne Carrick]] bluffed his way onto the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], hoping to warn Admiral Karath of a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] he had had of the Mandalorians [[Battle of Serroco|destroying]] the planet. Karath recognized him immediately, and had Viddarie place Carrick in [[Binders/Legends|binders]]. Viddarie had light [[Color/Legends|orange]] skin that was covered in small tentacle-like protrusions, and he had a tail for which a hole was cut in his Republic Navy uniform.<ref name="kotor15" />

==Behind the scenes==
Viddarie appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2007]]. In his sole appearance, Viddarie was illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]].<ref name="kotor15" />

==Appearances==
*[[Knights of the Old Republic 15|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 15]] {{1st}}
